steps.title: Send a Legal Notice
steps.description: Before filing a complaint, send a formal legal notice to the opposite party giving them 15-30 days to resolve the issue.

steps.title: Draft the Complaint
steps.description: Prepare the consumer complaint outlining facts, evidence, and relief sought.

steps.title: File the Complaint
steps.description: Submit the complaint to the appropriate Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission based on monetary jurisdiction.

steps.title: Notice to Vacate/Pay
steps.description: Serve a formal legal notice for eviction or pending rent payment.

steps.title: Filing Eviction Suit
steps.description: File a suit before the Rent Controller/Tribunal.

steps.title: Visit Police Station
steps.description: Go to the police station with jurisdiction over the incident area.

steps.title: Submit Written Complaint
steps.description: Submit a written complaint detailing the incident.

steps.title: Obtain FIR Copy
steps.description: Collect a free copy of the registered FIR.

steps.title: Identify Public Authority
steps.description: Determine which government department holds the information.

steps.title: Draft & Submit Application
steps.description: Write the RTI application and submit it with the prescribed fee (usually Rs. 10).

steps.title: Wait for Response
steps.description: The PIO is required to respond within 30 days.

steps.title: Demand letter
steps.description: Send a demand letter to the employer.

steps.title: Filing the Case
steps.description: If conciliation fails, file a case before the Labor Court/Industrial Tribunal.
